description_long: This is a twin-engine, high-wing airplane with a straight, rectangular wing configuration and a conventional tail assembly. The fuselage is relatively short and robust, with a forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit featuring multiple windows for visibility. The engines are mounted on the wings, each equipped with a propeller, indicating a turboprop configuration. The landing gear appears to be a tricycle type, with two main gear units under the wings and a nose gear under the cockpit. The exterior is finished in a light gray-green color with darker accents on the wings and tail, and the surface appears smooth and metallic. The airplane is designed for short takeoff and landing operations, suitable for regional or utility transport roles.
description: A twin-engine, high-wing turboprop airplane with a robust fuselage, tricycle landing gear, and a conventional tail, finished in light gray-green with dark accents.
description_view_0: Rear view showing the twin vertical stabilizers, high-mounted straight wings, and the tail section.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the forward cockpit, propeller engine, and the main landing gear under the wing.
description_view_2: Front view displaying the nose, cockpit windows, both propeller engines, and the full wingspan.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the cockpit, right engine, and the tail's angular design.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: High-wing, straight rectangular wings with no visible winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Twin turboprop engines mounted on the wings.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Tricycle landing gear with two main gear units and a single nose gear, fixed configuration.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with multiple windows, likely multi-seat.
